summary: react-campaign-optimizer postinstall exfiltrates host data to attacker-controlled requestrepo.com endpoint
details: On `npm install`, postinstall.js executes a lifecycle hook that uses child_process to run reconnaissance commands (whoami, curl, nslookup) and POSTs collected data — including os.hostname() output and other host identifiers — to a hardcoded attacker-controlled endpoint at https://2e3bkumw.requestrepo.com (requestrepo.com is a public request-inspection / exfiltration sink). The script also probes internal infrastructure such as https://kubernetes.default.svc, indicating intent to enumerate cloud/Kubernetes service-account context from inside the installer's environment. The combination of hardcoded C2 destination in install-time reachable code, host enumeration via shell commands, and posting host identifiers to that destination is an unambiguous credential/infrastructure exfiltration fingerprint.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
